title: Saskatchewan-China Exchange of Scholars Regulations, D-7.1 Reg 5

NOTE:
This consolidation is not official. Amendments have been 
incorporated for convenience of reference and the original statutes 
and regulations should be consulted for all purposes of interpretation 
and application of the law. In order to preserve the integrity of the 
original statutes and regulations, errors that may have appeared 
are reproduced in this consolidation.

CHAPTER D-7.1 REG 5
The Department of Advanced Education and Manpower Act
Title
1  These regulations may be cited as The Saskatchewan-China Exchange of Scholars 
Regulations.
Interpretation
2  In these regulations:
(a)  “China” means the People’s Republic of China;
(b)  “educational exchange agreement” means an agreement entered into 
by the minister on behalf of the Government of Saskatchewan and a province in 
China for the purpose of providing citizens of China with education or training 
opportunities in a post secondary institution;
(c)  “scholar” means a citizen of China:
(i)  who is studying in a post secondary institution; and
(ii)  whose name has been submitted to the minister;
in accordance with the terms of an educational exchange agreement.
27 Mar 86 cD-7.1 Reg 5 s2.
Grant
3(1)  Subject to subsections (2) to (4), where the minister is satisfied that an 
education or training opportunity offered by a post secondary institution is of benefit 
to a scholar and Saskatchewan, he may make a grant:
(a)  on behalf of the scholar enrolled in a post secondary institution to the 
institution; or
(b)  to the scholar.
(2)  The maximum amount of a grant under clause (1)(a) is equal to the cost of 
tuition, fees, books, educational supplies and room and board charged by the post 
secondary educational institution to provide the education or training program for 
the scholar.
(3)  The maximum amount of a grant under clause (1)(b) is equal to the cost of 
providing room and board not otherwise provided by the post secondary institution 
to the scholar while he is enrolled in the education or training program.
(4)  Where English as a second language training is considered necessary by the 
post secondary institution where the student is enrolled, no grant is payable to the 
scholar or on behalf of the scholar to the post secondary institution with respect to 
that training.
27 Mar 86 cD-7.1 Reg 5 s3.
